name: Conflicting Requirements — Anthropic Provider with Gemini-Only Feature
query: I want a Conductor workflow that calls Claude (`claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) with `googleSearchRetrieval: true` so it can ground its answers in Google Search results. Set it up.
expected_behavior: Step 1: Catch the conflict before generating the workflow. `googleSearchRetrieval` is a **Gemini-only** field (per references/workflow-definition.md and examples/llm-chat.md's built-in-tools table). It is silently ignored on Anthropic.

Step 2: Explain the conflict to the user in one or two sentences, plainly: 'You've combined an Anthropic model with a Gemini-only field. Anthropic doesn't support googleSearchRetrieval — the field will be silently dropped and Claude will answer without web grounding.'
Step 3: Offer the two valid paths and let the user pick:

- (a) **Keep Anthropic, swap to a feature it supports.** Use `webSearch: true` — Anthropic supports provider-native real-time web search. This is the right fix if 'I want Claude' is the firm requirement.

- (b) **Keep Google Search grounding, swap the provider to Gemini.** Use `llmProvider: google_gemini`, a Gemini thinking-capable model, and `googleSearchRetrieval: true`. This is the right fix if 'I want Google Search grounding' is the firm requirement.

Step 4: Briefly note the difference between the two paths so the user can pick — `webSearch` on Anthropic uses Anthropic's own web-search provider; `googleSearchRetrieval` on Gemini uses Google Search specifically. Not interchangeable in source.

Step 5: Do NOT generate the requested broken workflow as the primary deliverable. Either ask the user which path they want, OR generate both as labelled alternatives — but never silently ship the Anthropic + googleSearchRetrieval combination as if it works.
Step 6: Use Conductor's `{role, message}` schema (not `{role, content}`) in any workflow snippets shown.
Step 7: Reference the built-in-tools provider matrix from examples/llm-chat.md or references/workflow-definition.md when explaining what each provider supports.
success_criteria: Agent catches the conflict (Anthropic + googleSearchRetrieval) — does NOT silently produce the broken workflow as the recommended implementation
Agent correctly identifies `webSearch` as the Anthropic-compatible equivalent
Agent offers both valid alternative paths (keep Anthropic + use webSearch, OR keep googleSearchRetrieval + switch to Gemini) — does not unilaterally pick one
Agent explains the substantive difference between `webSearch` (Anthropic's own search) and `googleSearchRetrieval` (Google Search) so the user can make an informed pick
Any workflow JSON shown uses Conductor's `{role, message}` message schema (not `{role, content}`)
Agent does NOT invent a way to make Anthropic accept `googleSearchRetrieval` (no fake configuration, no claim that Conductor routes the call somehow) — the conflict is real and stated as real
